title: Which Of The Following Statements Is True Cyber Awareness 2022
source_filename: Which Of The Following Statements Is True Cyber Awareness 2022.docx

qas.q: (Spillage) What should you do if a reporter asks you about potentially classified information on the web?
qas.a: Refer the reporter to your organization's public affairs office.

qas.q: (Spillage) Which of the following is a good practice to aid in preventing spillage?
qas.a: Be aware of classification markings and all handling caveats.

qas.q: (Spillage) After reading an online story about a new security project being developed on the military installation where you work, your neighbor asks you to comment about the article. You know this project is classified. What should be your response?
qas.a: Attempt to change the subject to something non-work related, but neither confirm nor deny the article's authenticity.

qas.q: (Spillage) What should you do when you are working on an unclassified system and receive an email with a classified attachment?
qas.a: Call your security point of contact immediately.

qas.q: (Spillage) What is required for an individual to access classified data?
qas.a: Appropriate clearance; signed and approved non-disclosure agreement; and need-to-know.

qas.q: (Spillage) When classified data is not in use, how can you protect it?
qas.a: Store classified data appropriately in a GSA-approved vault/container.

qas.q: (Insider Threat) A colleague vacations at the beach every year, is married and a father of four, his work quality is sometimes poor, and he is pleasant to work with. How many potential insider threat indicators does this employee display?
qas.a: 0 indicators

qas.q: (Insider Threat) Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed? A colleague is playful and charming, consistently wins performance awards, and is occasionally aggressive in trying to access classified information.
qas.a: 1 indicators

qas.q: (Spillage) What type of activity or behavior should be reported as a potential insider threat?
qas.a: Coworker making consistent statements indicative of hostility or anger toward the United States and its policies.

qas.q: (Spillage) What advantages do "insider threats" have over others that allows them to cause damage to their organizations more easily?
qas.a: Insiders are given a level of trust and have authorized access to Government information systems.

qas.q: (Spillage) Which of the following is a best practice to protect information about you and your organization on social networking sites and applications?
qas.a: Use only personal contact information when establishing personal social networking accounts, never use Government contact information.

qas.q: (Spillage) When is the safest time to post details of your vacation activities on your social networking website?
qas.a: When your vacation is over, after you have returned home

qas.q: (social networking) When is the safest time to post details of your vacation activities on your social networking profile?
qas.a: After you have returned home following the vacation

qas.q: (Spillage) What level of damage can the unauthorized disclosure of information classified as confidential reasonably be expected to cause?
qas.a: Damage to national security

qas.q: (Spillage) Which type of information could reasonably be expected to cause serious damage to national security if disclosed without authorization?
qas.a: Secret

qas.q: (Spillage) Which of the following practices may reduce your appeal as a target for adversaries seeking to exploit your insider status?
qas.a: Remove your security badge after leaving your controlled area or office building.

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) What type of unclassified material should always be marked with a special handling caveat?
qas.a: For Official Use Only (FOUO)

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) Which of the following is NOT an example of sensitive information?
qas.a: Press release data

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) Which of the following is true about unclassified data?
qas.a: When unclassified data is aggregated, its classification level may rise.

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) Which of the following represents a good physical security practice?
qas.a: Use your own security badge, key code, or Common Access Card (CAC)/Personal Identity Verification (PIV) card.

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) What certificates are contained on the Common Access Card (CAC)?
qas.a: Identification, encryption, and digital signature

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) What should you do if a commercial entity, such as a hotel reception desk, asks to make a photocopy of your Common Access Card (CAC) for proof of Federal Government employment?
qas.a: Do not allow your CAC to be photocopied.

qas.q: (Sensitive Compartmented Information) What describes how Sensitive Compartmented Information is marked?
qas.a: Approved Security Classification Guide (SCG)

qas.q: (Sensitive Compartmented Information) Which of the following best describes the compromise of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
qas.a: A person who does not have the required clearance or assess caveats comes into possession of SCI in any manner.

qas.q: (Sensitive Compartmented Information) What portable electronic devices (PEDs) are allow in a Secure Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
qas.a: Government-owned PEDs, if expressly authorized by your agency.

qas.q: (Malicious Code) What are some examples of malicious code?
qas.a: Viruses, Trojan horses, or worms

qas.q: (Malicious Code) Which of the following is NOT a way that malicious code spreads?
qas.a: Legitimate software updates

qas.q: (Malicious Code) While you are registering for a conference, you arrive at the website http://www.dcsecurityconference.org/registration/. The website requires a credit card for registration. What should you do?
qas.a: Since the URL does not start with "https," do not provide your credit card information.

qas.q: (Malicious Code) Which email attachments are generally SAFE to open?
qas.a: Attachments contained in a digitally signed email from someone known

qas.q: (Malicious Code) What is a common indicator of a phishing attempt?
qas.a: It includes a threat of dire circumstances.

qas.q: (Malicious Code) Which of the following is true of Internet hoaxes?
qas.a: They can be part of a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack.

qas.q: (Malicious Code) Upon connecting your Government-issued laptop to a public wireless connection, what should you immediately do?
qas.a: Connect to the Government Virtual Private Network (VPN).

qas.q: (Malicious Code) A coworker has asked if you want to download a programmer's game to play at work. What should be your response?
qas.a: I'll pass

qas.q: (Malicious Code) What are some examples of removable media?
qas.a: Memory sticks, flash drives, or external hard drives

qas.q: (Malicious Code) Which are examples of portable electronic devices (PEDs)?
qas.a: laptops, fitness bands, tablets, smartphones, electric readers, and Bluetooth devices

qas.q: (Malicious Code) What is a good practice to protect data on your home wireless systems?
qas.a: Ensure that the wireless security features are properly configured.

qas.q: (social networking) When may you be subjected to criminal, disciplinary, and/or administrative action due to online misconduct?
qas.a: If you participate in or condone it at any time

qas.q: (social networking) Which of the following is a security best practice when using social networking sites?
qas.a: Use only personal contact information when establishing your personal account

qas.q: (controlled unclassified information) Which of the following is NOT an example of CUI?
qas.a: press release data

qas.q: (controlled unclassified information) Which of the following is NOT correct way to protect CUI?
qas.a: CUI may be stored on any password-protected system.

qas.q: (Physical Security) which Cyberspace Protection Condition (CPCON) establishes a protection priority focus on critical and essential functions only?
qas.a:
(Answer) CPCON 2 (High: Critical and Essential Functions)
-
CPCON 1 (Very High: Critical Functions)
CPCON 3 (Medium: Critical, Essential, and Support Functions)
CPCON 4 (Low: All Functions)
CPCON 5 (Very Low: All Functions)

qas.q: (Identity Management) What certificates are contained on the Common Access Card (CAC)?
qas.a: Identification, encryption, and digital signature

qas.q: (Identity Management) Which of the following is an example of two-factor authentication?
qas.a: Your password and the second commonly includes a text with a code sent to your phone

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) What guidance is available from marking Sensitive Information information (SCI)?
qas.a: Security Classification Guide (SCG)

qas.q: (Sensitive Information) What must the dissemination of information regarding intelligence sources, methods, or activities follow?
qas.a: The Director of National Intelligence.

qas.q: (removable media) If an incident occurs involving removable media in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF), what action should you take?
qas.a: Notify your security point of contact

qas.q: Which of the following actions can help to protect your identity?
qas.a: Order a credit report annually

qas.q: What is whaling?
qas.a:
Looking at your MOTHER, and screaming "THERE SHE BLOWS!!"
(A type of phishing targeted at senior officials)
Which is still your FAT A$$ MOTHER!

qas.q: Which is a best practice that can prevent viruses and other malicious code from being downloaded when checking your e-mail?
qas.a: Do not access website links, buttons, or graphics in e-mail

qas.q: What type of social engineering targets particular individuals, groups of people, or organizations?
qas.a: Spear phishing

qas.q: (Travel) Which of the following is a concern when using your Government-issued laptop in public?
qas.a: Others may be able to view your screen.

qas.q: (GFE) When can you check personal e-mail on your Government-furnished equipment (GFE)?
qas.a: If allowed by organizational policy

qas.q: (Mobile Devices) Which of the following statements is true?
qas.a: Mobile devices and applications can track your location without your knowledge or consent.

qas.q: (Mobile Devices) When can you use removable media on a Government system?
qas.a: When operationally necessary, owned by your organization, and approved by the appropriate authority

qas.q: (Home computer) Which of the following is best practice for securing your home computer?
qas.a: Create separate accounts for each user
